feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: The future of agentic processes: AI-driven enterprise automation
entry.content:
We talk a lot about AI agents.  But honestly… what does “agentic” really mean for your customers?

In this live partner session, we’ll unpack how SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Transformation Management combine to create something bigger than automation.

Imagine an AI agent that doesn’t just trigger a workflow—but understands the process, adapts, collaborates, and drives outcomes across the enterprise.  That’s the shift.  From task execution to end-to-end transformation.

We’ll bring it to life through real customer scenarios, the kind you can picture in your own pipeline.  You’ll see how architecture, process intelligence, and AI come together in practical, scalable ways.

And yes, we’ll keep it grounded.  Clear use cases.  Tangible value.  No buzzword overload.

If you’re helping customers navigate transformation, this session will give you sharper tools—and stronger stories.

Join us.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012906.html

#SAP #SAPCommunity #SAPBTP #BusinessProcessTransformation #AgenticAI #SAPPartners

entry.title: Leveraging data product studio - what is coming
entry.content:
Ever had a customer say, “We have the data… we just can’t use it”?

That’s exactly where Leveraging data product studio – what is coming comes in.

Inside SAP Business Data Cloud, the data product studio empowers data engineers to create reusable, business-ready data products.  Think of it like building Lego blocks for analytics and AI—modular, governed, and designed to scale.

In this live session, we’ll unpack:
• Why customers are rethinking their data foundations
• What the architecture actually looks like (no buzzwords, real walkthrough)
• How partners can guide adoption—and lead the opportunity

Honestly, this isn’t just another feature update. It’s a shift in how we help customers innovate faster and extend intelligent applications with confidence.

If you’re shaping strategy, selling value, or delivering transformation, you’ll want to be in this room.

Register now and be part of the next chapter.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/oct/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00011695.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPBDC #SAPPartners #DataInnovation #BusinessDataCloud #AIReadyData

entry.title: SAP BTP AI best practices - SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Still extracting document data manually? Let’s change that.

In our next SAP BTP AI best practices webinar, we spotlight SAP Document AI — and how it automates extraction of structured and unstructured information on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Invoices.  Contracts.  Forms.  Done.
Machine learning + generative AI + instant learning.  Even better.

This isn’t theory.  It’s practical guidance you can use immediately — in customer demos, project delivery, or new AI proposals.  The opportunity is real, and partners who master document intelligence early will stand out.

Short session.  High impact.  Real use cases.

Secure your spot.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/nov/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012198.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPDocumentAI #AIUseCases #SAPPartners #Innovation

entry.title: Live Demos at the SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable – 03 March 2026
entry.content:
Join our next SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable on 03.03.2026 (10:00–11:00 CET) 

Packed with live demos and product insights:

🔹Fiori Elements development with building blocks (Katrin Polloczek & Marcel Wächter)

🔹New My Home (Caroline Welsh)

🔹Product Updates (Frank Jentsch)

The roundtable is a monthly series taking place on the first Tuesday of every month (10:00–11:00 CET).

👉Download the .ics calendar file for the full 2026 series and import it directly into your calendar.
Each calendar entry already includes the Microsoft Teams meeting link.

Microsoft Teams meeting link (valid for the entire 2026 series): Microsoft Teams meeting link 

All agendas, slides, recordings, and updates from past and current sessions are published centrally via the
SAP Community – SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable.

We’re looking forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Comprehensive Overview: Analyze ESG Data Application in SAP Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Disclaimer: This blog post reflects the state of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower as of February 17th, 2026.

The Analyze ESG Data application in SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) has evolved into a central workspace for exploring and reporting sustainability data. Recent enhancements – such as configurable drilldown dimensions, public analytics configurations, extended period selection, and flexible export options – make it easier than ever for users to navigate ESG metrics, create consistent views, and share insights across teams. This overview summarizes the key capabilities that support faster, clearer, and more collaborative sustainability reporting and provides a practical, user-focused supplement to the SAP Help Portal documentation.

Getting Started: “My Views” & Filters

  • The application requires users to select a reporting period and structure before displaying data, ensuring relevant and targeted analysis:

 

  • Once you have selected the relevant Period and Reporting Structure you can also save this using the “My Views” functionality and set it as default. These views are only visible to you, and don't affect other users.

Manage Overviews: Customization and Collaboration

  • The “Overview” dashboard organizes metrics into groups, which are defined in the “Manage Overviews” section. Users can switch between public and private overviews, edit groupings, and add or remove metrics to tailor the dashboard to their needs.
  • Public overviews allow teams to share standardized metric groupings, while private overviews support individual customization.
  • Metrics can be reordered within groups for better visibility, and the system provides clear feedback when no data matches the selected filters. 

Analytics: Deep Dive, Drill-Downs, and Shared Views

  • Users can access the “Analytics” tab by selecting a metric for a prefilled view or starting from scratch via the analytics tab, supporting both guided and exploratory analysis. 

Option 1: Selecting a metric from the “Overview” page:

Option 2: Select the metric within “Analytics” tab

  • Independent whether you clicked on the metric from the “Overview” tab or you selected it directly within the “Analytics” tab, the metric is visualized with a grouping by “Period” per default (if nothing else is applied).
  • Public and private analytics views allow admins and data managers to predefine drill-down configurations for each(!) metric, which can be set as defaults for all users. This ensures consistency in reporting and facilitates knowledge sharing across teams. 

  • The “Manage Analytics” section provides a centralized location to view and edit all saved analytics configurations, with public views editable by authorized users. Any public view can be edited by anyone with the required authorizations.

Advanced Filtering, Drill-Downs, and Export

  • Users can filter and drill down by multiple dimensions, such as period, reporting structure, and energy source type, with up to five drill-downs per metric. This flexibility supports detailed analysis and custom reporting. 

  • Within the “View Settings” you can also create additional filters by “Period”, “Reporting Structure” or “Drill-down Dimension”. Please note that filters within “View Settings” overwrite settings in “My Views” if applied. For filtering within “View Setting” there is a current limit of 12 nodes for multi-selection in hierarchies, set for performance and visualization reasons. Depending on customer feedback this limit might be expanded. 

  • If filtering is applied via the “View Settings” and “My Views” is overwritten, this is also highlighted as an info:

  • Export functionality enables users to download chart data as PNG images and tables as Excel files, making it easy to share insights and integrate with other reporting tools. 

  • Drill-downs for each metric can be set up in the “Manage Metrics” application for up to 5 drill-down dimensions. Select the relevant drill-down dimension(s) and by clicking on saving, this directly calculates the drill downs. Once it has been calculated and saved, the drill-downs can then directly be visualized in the “Analytics” tab of the Analyze ESG Data application

Upcoming Features & Further Resources

  • Key priorities for upcoming releases include displaying targets within the “Analytics” tab, enabling multi-selection in the global filter for reporting structures, support for more chart types with saved custom chart views and saving “Analytics” settings for the whole page across multiple charts further enhancing flexibility and usability. 
  • If you are driving a project with SCT, please upvote or submit improvement requests via SAP Influence to ensure that future updates align with real-world needs and use cases. 
  • For further details, consult the SAP Help Portal and the SAP Roadmap for SCT:
    • Using the Analyze ESG Data Application | SAP Help Portal
    • SAP SCT Roadmap
    • Don’t forget to subscribe the What’s New section within SAP Help:
      What's New in SAP Sustainability Control Tower | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Getting Started with the cTMS Catalog in SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to the world of SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Cloud Transport Management service – two powerful tools from the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) DevOps portfolio. In this blog post, we'll explore how these innovative services work together to transform your DevOps processes. You'll discover how SAP Automation Pilot's commands can seamlessly integrate with SAP Cloud Transport Management, automating tasks like managing transport nodes, routes, and requests. Join us as we guide you through leveraging these tools to streamline your software delivery, enhance operational efficiency, and ensure smoother deployments across your SAP environments.

What is SAP Automation Pilot?

SAP Automation Pilot is a cloud-based service within SAP BTP that streamlines DevOps processes. This low-code/no-code engine offers over 300 pre-built commands for various DevOps tasks, while also allowing users to create custom commands. Highly scalable and integrating seamlessly with SAP services and third-party tools, SAP Automation Pilot empowers teams to reduce operational overhead and enhance efficiency across their technology stack. More details about the product can be found at:

  • SAP Discovery Center

  • SAP Automation Pilot Documentation

What is SAP Cloud Transport Management?

SAP Cloud Transport Management service (cTMS) simplifies the movement of software deliverables across different environments and runtimes. It manages the transport of both application artifacts and content, supporting various platforms like Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Neo. The service enhances transparency by providing a detailed audit trail of changes, while also enabling a clear separation of duties between development and operations teams. More details about the product can be found at:

  • SAP Cloud Transport Management Documentation

Introducing the SAP Automation Pilot cTMS Catalog

We're excited to share how these two powerful tools work together to transform your DevOps processes. Through collaborative efforts, we've developed 16 specialized commands for a dedicated cTMS catalog within SAP Automation Pilot. These new commands cover essential functions including transport nodes, transport routes, and transport requests. This integration enables automatic triggering of specific cTMS tasks, paving the way for fully automated, end-to-end workflows. Whether you're managing SAP projects or looking to streamline your transport management processes, this powerful synergy will help you boost efficiency, reduce errors, and take your DevOps practices to the next level. More details about the catalog and its full set of commands can be found at:

  • SAP Help Portal

 

Step-by-Step Guide

Prerequisites:

  • You have an active SAP BTP account. See Get started with SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • You've set up your global account and at least one subaccount on SAP BTP.  See Getting Started in the Cloud Foundry Environment.
  • SAP Automation Pilot. See Initial Setup.
  • You've set up SAP Automation Pilot in your subaccount. See Initial Setup.
  • You're a global account administrator.
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management. See Prerequisites.

After you’ve made sure that you meet all the prerequisites, you can proceed with the steps listed below.

  1. Access SAP Automation Pilot through your subaccount. Once inside, go to the Inputs section and choose Create.

     

  2. Fill the required fields. Choose a catalog from the dropdown menu, and give your input a descriptive name. Choose Create.

     

  3. Get your SAP Cloud Transport Management service key/binding - access your subaccount, navigate to Instances and Subscriptions > Instances > Credentials, choose service binding, and Copy JSON.


     

  4. Go back to the Inputs tab in SAP Automation Pilot and choose Add. In the dialog that appears, fill the required fields. For Type choose Object, turn on the Sensitive toggle, and provide the cTMS service key or binding in the Value section. Choose Add.

     

     

  5. In SAP Automation Pilot go to the Provided Catalogs section. From there, locate the Cloud Transport Management Service (ctms-sapcp) catalog and choose Commands.

     

  6. On the catalog page, you'll see a list of 16 new commands for managing cTMS resources. These commands allow you to manage transport nodes, transport routes, and transport requests. Choose Create.

     

  7. Fill the required fields. Choose a catalog from the dropdown menu, and give your command a descriptive name. Choose Create.

     

  8. Proceed by choosing Add under input key section. In the form that appears, fill the required fields. For Type choose Object, turn on the Sensitive toggle, under Input choose the one created in Step 2. Choose Add.

     

     

  9. To configure your command, navigate to the Executors section and choose Add.

     

  10. Fill the required fields. Choose CreateTransportNode Command from the dropdown menu. Choose Add.

     

  11. Scroll down to Parameters and choose Edit. Fill the required fields as demonstrated:

    • forwardMode: determines when transport requests will be forwarded. Select one of the following predefined values: Pre-Import, Post-Import, On Success, Manual

    • name: give the node a descriptive name

    • serviceKey: use the name previously provided as InputKey in Step 8

       

  12. Create a second transport node by choosing Duplicate.

     

  13. Scroll down to Parameters and choose Edit. Be sure to rename the new transport node to differentiate it from the previous one.

     

  14. Repeat Step 10, choosing CreateTransportRoute Command from the dropdown menu. Scroll down to Parameters and choose Edit. Fill the required fields as demonstrated:

    • name: give the route a descriptive name

    • serviceKey: use the name previously provided as InputKey in Step 8

    • sourceNodeId: ID of the source transport node

    • targetNodeId: ID of the target transport node

       

  15. After you have done that, trigger the command.

     

  16. Upon successful execution, you should see your nodes and route displayed in cTMS.

     

     

Wrap-up

In this guide, we've explored the powerful integration between SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Cloud Transport Management, demonstrating how these tools can revolutionize your DevOps processes. By leveraging the new cTMS catalog in Automation Pilot, you can now automate complex transport management tasks with ease. This integration not only enhances efficiency but also reduces the potential for errors in your software delivery pipeline. As you implement these practices, you'll find yourself well-equipped to handle the challenges of modern DevOps in SAP environments, ensuring smoother deployments and more reliable operations.

entry.title: Reference architectures for developing custom AI solutions in SAP BTP
entry.content:
AI is powerful.  Architecture makes it scalable.

In our live session, “Reference architectures for developing custom AI solutions in SAP Business Technology Platform,” we’ll cut through the noise and focus on what matters:

  • AI capabilities across SAP
  • SAP Business AI and Joule in action
  • Custom AI development options
  • Proven reference architectures and use cases

The thing is, customers don’t just want AI features.  They want outcomes, automated processes, smarter decisions, measurable impact.  And that starts with the right foundation in SAP BTP.

If you’re driving sales, shaping solutions, or delivering implementations, this session will sharpen your AI playbook.

Let’s move from experimentation to enterprise-grade execution.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012763.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPBusinessAI #Joule #SAPEnablement #AIInnovation

entry.title: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content:
Introduction

Last year, I got an opportunity to work on a project where we had to build  multi-tenant APIs on SAP BTP.  The real-life constraints were the interesting part: multiple customers, strict data isolation, centralized lifecycle management, and the need to keep operations as simple as possible. This experience inspired me to learn more about multi-tenancy, build a standalone prototype and understand the design. I created a full stack application that demonstrates multi-tenancy with CAP, MTX sidecar, SaaS provisioning and tenant-specific persistence. In this blog, I will walk you through the core concepts, the architecture and the concrete steps to get you started.

What is multi-tenancy?

Before touching any code, it’s important to understand what multi-tenancy actually means in the SAP BTP context. In simple terms, multi-tenancy means that a single running instance of your application serves multiple customers (tenants), while keeping their data strictly isolated. The below diagram gives a simple representation of how multi-tenant applications are different from single tenant applications

 Figure-1

 We usually talk about two perspectives:

  1. Provider: The global account owner who develops and operates the SaaS solution.​

  2. Subscriber: The customer (or department) that subscribes to the provider’s application and has its own users and identity provider.

In a multitenant architecture:

  1. One deployed app and shared platform resources serve many subscribers, which optimizes compute usage.​

  2. Tenant-specific data is stored in separate schemas or containers so that each tenant only sees its own data.​

  3. The platform (via services like XSUAA, Service Manager, SaaS Registry, and CAP’s MTX) helps route requests and resolve the correct tenant database at runtime.

In the prototype, I have created a full stack application (following standard CAP layout), but the same principles apply to any real-world business scenario.

How to achieve it on SAP BTP?

​In the prototype, multi-tenancy is achieved by combining - CAP, the MTX microservice and several BTP services such as XSUAA, Service Manager and SaaS Registry. Conceptually, there are three key stages: provider deployment, subscriber onboarding and tenant-specific runtime request handling.

1. Provider subaccount – deployment and setup

On the provider subaccount, you deploy your CAP application along with the multi-tenancy infrastructure. The below diagram show cases the provider subaccount perspective

Figure-2

  1. The CAP application (srv + UI) and the App Router are deployed as Cloud Foundry apps from an MTA archive.​

  2. Cloud Foundry deploy also provisions the XSUAA instance (tenant mode - shared) and a Service Manager instance to orchestrate tenant-specific database provisioning.​

  3. An MTX sidecar microservice is deployed as a separate CF app to handle subscription, onboarding and off-boarding of tenants.​

  4. A SaaS registry instance is configured with callback URLs pointing to the MTX sidecar’s endpoints (for example: onSubscription and getDependencies).​

  5. A technical tenant (often referred to as - t0) is prepared to store tenant registry and runtime metadata.

This setup turns the CAP project into a SaaS-ready multi-tenant application on BTP. 

2. Subscriber subaccount – subscription and tenant provisioning

When a customer subscribes to the application from their own subaccount, the platform and MTX collaborate to create tenant-specific resources. The below diagram show cases the subscriber subaccount perspective

Figure-3 

  1. The subscriber initiates a subscription to the provider’s SaaS app from the Service Marketplace.​

  2. SaaS Registry calls the MTX sidecar callbacks: first getDependency (if any dependent services are needed), then onSubscription to start onboarding.​

  3. MTX registers the tenant in t0 and then triggers Service Manager to provision the tenant’s database artifacts (for example, a dedicated HDI container/schema and binding).​

  4. The CAP model is deployed into this new tenant schema using the deployment and model provider services.​

  5. Finally, a tenant-specific URL is generated and stored so that the subscriber can access the app via their own hostname.

Note: An important operational step is mapping the subscriber hostname as an additional route to the provider’s App Router in the provider space, so that tenant-specific URLs resolve correctly. This is outlined in the GitHub repository as well.

3. Runtime – What happens when a user opens the tenant URL

Once subscriptions are in place, the multi-tenant behavior becomes visible at runtime. The below diagram show cases the subscriber subaccount perspective

Figure-4

  1. A user accesses the subscriber-specific URL which is routed to the provider’s App Router.​

  2. The App Router inspects the host/subdomain, derives the tenant identifier and initiates authentication with the shared XSUAA instance.​

  3. XSUAA delegates authentication to the subscriber’s IdP (for example, IAS or a corporate IdP) and returns an authorization code to the App Router.​

  4. The App Router exchanges this code for a JWT access token and forwards requests to the CAP backend with that token.​

  5. CAP resolves the tenant from the JWT and looks up the corresponding tenant-specific DB binding via MTX and Service Manager.

  6. Application executes the request against that tenant’s schema or HDI container and data is presented to the user.

From the user’s perspective, they simply use the subscriber specific URL and see the data, while the provider operates a single shared application landscape.

Git repository

The full prototype which includes the CAP project, MTX sidecar configuration and step-by-step instructions is available in the GitHub repository The repository contains:​

  1. A standard CAP layout project created using Joule For Developers.​

  2. Multi-tenancy configuration in package.json, mta.yaml, xs-security.json and xs-app.json.​

  3. The mtx-sidecar microservice used to manage tenant lifecycle operations.​

  4. Detailed README with architecture diagrams, local testing and BTP deployment steps.

You can clone the repository, enhance the domain model and adapt the setup to your own multi-tenant scenarios on SAP BTP.

Conclusion

Designing and operating a multi-tenant SaaS application on SAP BTP can look complex at first, but CAP and MTX abstract much of the heavy lifting behind a clean programming model and well-integrated platform services.

By combining CAP’s multi-tenancy features with XSUAA, Service Manager, SaaS Registry and Cloud Foundry, you can run a single codebase that securely serves multiple customers with isolated data. The prototype shows the journey from local testing (with mocked tenants) to a full BTP setup with provider and subscriber accounts, subscriptions and tenant-specific URLs. 

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/
  2. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/multitenancy/#enable-multitenancy
  3. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/developing-multitenant-applications-in-cloud-foundry-environment

entry.title: Building multitenant SaaS solutions with SAP Build: A partner's guide to innovate for scale
entry.content:
Building a SaaS solution is exciting.  Building one that scales cleanly across customers without breaking the core?  That’s where things get interesting.

In our upcoming webinar, “Building multitenant SaaS solutions with SAP Build: A partner's guide to innovate for scale,” we’ll walk through how you can design, deploy, and commercialize multitenant applications on SAP Business Technology Platform while staying true to clean core principles with SAP Build.

We’ll cover the real journey, from architecture and ERP-agnostic design to pricing models, provisioning, and cost structures for dev, test, demo, and production.  Plus, we’ll connect the dots between technical decisions and commercial impact.

Honestly, multitenancy isn’t just a technical choice.  It’s a business multiplier.  One code line.  Multiple customers.  Controlled extensions.  Stronger margins.

Whether you’re in the SAP PartnerEdge Open Ecosystem or a Build partner refining your portfolio, this session is designed to give you clarity and confidence.

Register now and start building for scale, the smart way.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013076.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPBuild #SaaS #SAPPartners #CleanCore

entry.title: SAP Commodity Management: Driving the Bottom Line with Precision Expense Control
entry.content:
In commodity trading, third-party expenses are not merely overheads; they are critical factors that significantly influence the profitability of trading activities. Costs such as demurrage, brokerage, throughput charges, insurance, and tank cleaning, when not managed precisely, can lead to margin and revenue leakage. While SAP’s native capabilities offer robust expense management, the unique and dynamic requirements of commodity supply chains, including the need to track costs throughout the entire deal and contract lifecycle, necessitate a specialized approach.

To address these industry-specific challenges, we are thrilled to have launched SAP Commodity Expense Management, as part of S/4HANA 2025. This new product is purpose-built as an application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), designed specifically for commodity organizations to provide focused, industry-relevant expense management, all supported by dedicated and intuitive Fiori Apps delivering enhanced usability.

The solution delivers several key benefits:

  • Increased Automation: Leverages automation rules defined in Expense Master Data, automatic defaulting of expenses during planning and automated accrual reversals and adjustments.
  • Enhanced Integration and Visibility: Provides cross-company visibility of expenses across multiple document types from capture to settlement.
  • Improved Data Accuracy: Reduces the risk of margin and revenue leakage through better expense planning and integrated reporting.

SAP Commodity Expense Management offers a comprehensive suite of capabilities, ensuring granular control and enhanced visibility over commodity-related costs across both Energy and Agri industries:

  • Expense Setup & Configuration: This feature provides tools to define the fundamental structure of your expenses. It allows for the setup of various expense types, units of measure (UoMs), currencies, and account mapping. Users can configure automation rules, mass update expense rates, and maintain essential posting assignments for both accrual and settlement processes. Furthermore, the solution offers extensibility with flexibility to define attributes for determining templates and expense rates, along with APIs for mass upload of these rates.
  • Expense Planning: Essential for proactive cost management, this capability supports both automatic and manual expense planning. Expenses can be planned during deal capture, contract creation, nomination, and Agricultural Contract Management  Application Document items, allowing for iterative expense updates throughout the process. The system facilitates defaulting of expenses based on predefined automation rules and allows tracking of overrides and manual additions at various stages. It offers the ability to determine expenses using customer-specific attributes during Deal Capture.
  • Expense Accrual: This function handles the recognition of liabilities for services received. Accruals can be created and adjusted based on key events such as Deal Publish, Contract Approval, Nomination Confirmation, and ACM Application Documents/Ticketing. It includes features for automatic accrual reversals, automatic accrual on ticket corrections, and the ability to split and collate accruals for complex scenarios. Accrual postings are integrated during delivery execution.
  • Expense Settlement: Designed to streamline the payment process, this feature supports invoice verification and the settlement of both planned and unplanned expenses, distinct from core commodity contract settlements. It allows for correction of service provider and invoice amounts and provides for the recovery of expenses from contract or deal counterparties. An API is available to capture Expense Settlement, and it includes tax code determination for accurate financial postings. Settlement postings are integrated into the accounting process.

By bringing precision, automation, and comprehensive visibility to third-party costs, SAP Commodity Expense Management empowers organizations to transform expenses from a potential liability into a strategic advantage. Don’t let unmanaged costs erode your profitability; embrace the future of commodity expense control. We invite you to explore the capabilities further by visiting the SAP Help Portal and Roadmap Explorer (links below).

Visit SAP Help Portal for more an Administration Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/CCS/7b6b503b6ae0446fb3d6801c98f56b1e/2fe3a2ae04e74a598c8c39f73b4710e3.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Stay tuned for future innovations by following SAP Roadmap Explorer  for Commodity Management: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=42F2E964F4E71EDAA6A3587248CD40E5&range=CURRENT-LAST

entry.title: Unlocking the business value of SAP Business Data Cloud - BDC value framework
entry.content:
Features are easy.  Value wins deals.

In “Unlocking the business value of SAP Business Data Cloud – BDC value framework,” we go straight to what matters: turning SAP Business Data Cloud into a quantified, strategic business case.

You’ll get:
✔ A structured Value Framework linking capabilities to real business drivers
✔ A Value Calculator to put numbers behind the story
✔ A maturity assessment that exposes process gaps and sparks opportunity

No fluff.  Just practical tools you can use in your very next customer conversation.

The difference between “interesting” and “compelling” often comes down to one thing: clarity around impact. Let’s sharpen that edge together.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013036.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPEcosystem #SAPBDC #PartnerSuccess #ValueSelling #BusinessTransformation

entry.title: Harnessing SAP BW data product generator for BW modernization with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Many of our customers have invested years in building robust SAP BW landscapes.  The question we keep hearing is: how do we modernize without starting from scratch?

In our upcoming webinar, Harnessing SAP BW data product generator for BW modernization with SAP Business Data Cloud, we’ll explore how the SAP BW data product generator, part of SAP Business Data Cloud, helps you transform existing BW assets into reusable data products, quickly and intelligently.

Think of it as turning well-organized shelves in BW into ready-to-serve data packages for cloud innovation.  No heavy lifting.  No wasted value.

We’ll walk through prerequisites, roles, system requirements, plus a live demo showing exactly how to create and activate data products step by step.  And yes, we’ll leave space for real questions in an open Q&A.

If you’re in pre-sales, delivery, or architecture, this session will help you accelerate BW modernization conversations with confidence.

Register now and secure your spot.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010566.html

#SAP #SAPPartners #SAPBusinessDataCloud #SAPBW #DataModernization #SAPBTP

entry.title: Master Blog: B2B Integration with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction 

B2B integration plays a critical role in enabling seamless and reliable data exchange between enterprises and their business partners. As integration landscapes grow in scale and complexity, standardized B2B capabilities become essential to ensure secure connectivity, partner onboarding, message validation, monitoring, and operational resilience.  

This master blog serves as a technical reference hub for B2B Integration Factory, consolidating all related blog posts in one place to support integration architects and developers in designing, implementing, and operating scalable and governed B2B integration scenarios with SAP Integration Suite. 

If you are interested in deepening your knowledge of B2B integration using SAP Integration Suite, we recommend our free learning course. This comprehensive course offers detailed explanations of each B2B capability and includes hands-on exercises with an example end-to-end B2B scenario. You can access it here: SAP Learning: Manage B2B Scenarios Effectively with SAP Integration Suite. 

 

B2B Integration Factory 

B2B integration Factory’s services & integration packages 

  • B2B Integration Factory services to unlock the power of SAP Integration Suite's B2B capabilities 
  • B2B Integration Factory – B2B related Integration Packages with your Custom Extensions

  • B2B Integration Factory's Role and Value Adds in t... - SAP Community 
  • B2B Integration Factory Q4/2024 Release highlights - SAP Community 
  • First release of B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages and their Extensions 
  • SAP Migration Factory for Integration Suite – FAQ - SAP Community 
  • The importance of B2B Monitoring in customer's day... - SAP Community 
  • VDA Automotive Flat File Processing with B2B Integ... - SAP Community 

 

B2B integration scenarios  

Blog posts describing end-to-end B2B integration scenarios using IA, TPM and CPI 

  • B2B data exchange using Trading Partner Management... - SAP Community 
  • B2B Scenario Sender EDI to IDOC - SAP Community 
  • Bridging EDI and SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Seamless EDI-t... - SAP Community 
  • Completing the B2B migration into SAP Integration ... - SAP Community 
  • Creating MIGs and MAGs Using Integration Advisor i... - SAP Community 
  • How to build custom B2B Interface using Integratio... - SAP Community 
  • Inbound Interface: EDI → SAP BTP (Integration Suit... - SAP Community 
  • Real-Time XML to EDI Conversion: A Step-by-Step CP... - SAP Community 
  • SAP BTP - Integration Advisor - (MIG & MAG) from a... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Integration Suite - TPM - B2B PassThrough Scen... - SAP Community 
  • Streamlining B2B Integration with AI-Powered SAP C... - SAP Community 
  • Use TPM, IA, CI to Efficiently Manage and Run Comp... - SAP Community 

 

Integration Advisor 

Blog posts focusing only on IA 

  • Beginner's Guide to Integration Advisor - SAP Community 
  • Base-/Overlay Mappings for Integration Advisor - SAP Community 
  • Creating MIGs and MAGs Using Integration Advisor i... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Automotive EDIFACT Subsets (... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – change in namespace handling... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor: complex mapping code for dime... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Create 1:1 Mapping Elements ... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Download MAG Simulation Resu... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – GS1 XML Messages and Namespa... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – GS1 XML Messages of the vers... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – direct support of EDI payloa... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor: Early UN/EDIFACT Versions wit... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – extended support for Custom ... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Extended semantic validation... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor: Functions at target side - Or... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor - List of supported XSLT eleme... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Mapping element functions, t... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Mapping element functions fo... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – MAG: How to create If-Then-E... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – MIG Qualification based on m... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – multi-payload support for MI... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – new B2B Library content for ... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Now GS1 EANCOM Library avail... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Odette Message Standard - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor: Overview of components for bu... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – payload-based qualification ... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor - Quickly create your individu... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor - support for multi-value Qual... - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Support of xCBL Messages - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Tradacoms Message Standard - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor – Use Custom XSLT as MAG - SAP Community 
  • Integration Advisor: Using the Pre-Transformation ... - SAP Community 
  • integration content advisor: Create a customized i... - SAP Community  
  • integration content advisor: Create a mapping usin... - SAP Community  
  • integration content advisor: The importance of cus... - SAP Community 
  • How to Provide Read-only Access to Integration Adv... - SAP Community 
  • Restoring the hierarchical order of a flattened so... - SAP Community 
  • SAP BTP - IS - ( IA | CPI ) - The Commander's Blue... - SAP Community 
  • Support for ASC X12 Subsets (VICS etc.) in Integra... - SAP Community 
  • Streamlining B2B Integration with AI-Powered SAP C... - SAP Community 
  • Taming the X12 856 message in SAP Integration Advi... - SAP Community 
  • Using EDI Envelopes in Integration Advisor - SAP Community 

 

Trading Partner Management 

Blog posts focusing only on TPM 

  • Archiving B2B Messages from SAP Integration Suite ... - SAP Community 
  • B2B data exchange using Trading Partner Management... - SAP Community 
  • Custom Message Mapping - TPM - B2B - SAP Integrati... - SAP Community 
  • Custom Search Attributes in SAP B2B - SAP Community 
  • Integrating Cloud Application Lifecyle Management ... - SAP Community 
  • Guide for migrating PI/PO B2B mappings to Integrat... - SAP Community 
  • Reusing SAP PI/PO B2B mapping content in Cloud Int... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Cloud Integration : B2B - Restart/Retry Messag... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Trading Partner Management - Part I: TPM overv... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Trading Partner Management - Part II: Data - SAP Community 
  • SAP Trading Partner Management - Part III: Partner... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Trading Partner Management - Part IV: Agreemen... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management... - SAP Community 
  • Troubleshooting SAP BTP TPM: Debugging Step 1b and... - SAP Community 
  • Using Trading Partner Management with AI - SAP Community 

 

Summary 

This collection brings together key insights, best practices, and technical deep dives on B2B integration using SAP Integration Factory, helping you design, implement, and optimize your B2B integration landscape. If you feel a relevant topic or blog post is missing, please let us know in the comments—we’re happy to expand the list and keep this collection current and comprehensive.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2602
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2602

 
On February 14, the new 2602 release was delivered as the successor to the 2511 release. In this blog post, we provide a detailed overview of the new features and enhancements included in this release, highlighting the key improvements and innovations introduced with 2602. Some highlights in the new release include the My Home as your new personalized entry page in SAP Fiori Launchpad, the CDS Embedded Analytics Star Schema Generator and the RAP recommendations for input assistance based on the user input and interaction history. 

The list of all new features including more information can be found on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP environment page. The new AI-related features are listed separately on the What's New for ABAP Generative AI in ABAP Cloud.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • New Grouping Option Effect on transports in ATC Problems View
  • ABAP Testability: Enhancements to Test Double Frameworks (details)
  • Global User ID for ATC Exemptions
  • Displaying the Used Objects of CDS Service Definitions in the Relation Explorer

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP)

  • Collaborative Draft,  now also works in cross-business object scenarios if both business objects are collaborative draft-enabled, and if the cross-business object scenario is fully draft scope-enabled (details)
  • Recommendations, previously, business users had to fill out input fields manually and choose the right values. The new RAP recommendations standardize and ease this process: In RAP applications on an SAP Fiori user interface, RAP recommendations provide input assistance by proposing field values for input fields based on the user input and interaction history. RAP recommendations can be implemented using deterministic rules or using generative AI, which predicts the most likely values for each input field based on the context of the data. By integrating the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management, you can embed AI capabilities into the application, enriching traditional value help with context-aware suggestions (details)
  • Embedded Analytics Star Schema Generator, the Star Schema Generator Wizard streamlines the creation of star schema models using ABAP Core Data Services. It empowers you to build comprehensive analytical models with ease. This tool guides you through the entire generation process, ensuring a consistent and efficient workflow. The wizard supports the following key components: Transactional Scenario, Analytical Cube and Dimension Views (details)

Identity and Access Management

  • IAM App Assignment: So far, it was only possible to assign access to Fiori apps to business users indirectly via business catalogs. Now, it's also possible to directly create business roles based on IAM apps for SAP-delivered business catalogs
  • Maintain Business Roles, you can now directly assign IAM apps in the Maintain Business Roles app. This supports you in handling authorizations more flexibly. You can also use the Mass Change Wizard if you need to add or remove multiple IAM apps at once (details)
  • App References, the following apps now contain references to IAM apps: Maintain Business Roles, Manage Business Role, Changes After Upgrade, Display Restriction Types, Business Catalogs, Business Role Templates, IAM Information System (details)
  • Information System, the main entity Application is now deprecated. The successor will be the IAM app, which is now contained in the Main Entity dropdown list (details)
  • App Authorization Variant, you can now use the IAM app of the App Authorization Variant type. This gives you more flexibility in managing restrictions because it allows you to activate or deactivate app authorization variants to define different access rights per business role (details)

Manage API Snapshots

  • As a provider for released APIs, you can now download and upload API snapshots using the Manage API Snapshots app. This way, you can download API snapshots from the test system and upload them in the development system, thus preventing incompatible changes to your released objects early in the development process (details)

SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • My Home in SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • Launching an App with a Transaction Code (details)
  • Download and Upload of Spaces 
  • Removal of Business Groups 

Landscape Portal

  • Publish Product: Publish up to Two Product Versions (details)
  • Deploy Product: System multi-select supported and performance improved (details)

Infrastructure

  • Optional Integration with SAP Key Management Service, SAP BTP ABAP environment provides an optional integration with the SAP Key Management Service (KMS). With this integration, it's possible to manage the SAP HANA Cloud database of an ABAP environment instance via SAP KMS (details)

entry.title: Best practices and guidelines in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Let’s be real.  Integration can either feel like elegant orchestration… or controlled chaos.

That’s why we’re hosting “Best practices and guidelines in SAP Integration Suite”.  This session goes beyond theory.  We’ll explore how SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) fits into modern enterprise architecture and how to apply recommended integration patterns across SAP-to-SAP, SAP-to-non-SAP, and hybrid scenarios.

Plus, we’ll dive into the details that actually matter in projects: secure connectivity, smart scripting, message processing, monitoring, and alerting.  The stuff that keeps your integrations stable at 2 a.m. when no one wants surprises.

Think of it like designing highways between systems.  Good design means smooth traffic. Poor design?  Bottlenecks everywhere.

If you’re building or optimizing integrations for customers, this session will give you clarity and confidence.

Register now and let’s build integrations that scale with purpose.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013114.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPIntegrationSuite #SAPCloudIntegration #SAPPartners #DigitalTransformation #HybridIntegration

entry.title: Where Your Ideas Shape SAP BTP: Become Part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2026-1
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform is reshaping how organizations innovate. By unifying application development, automation, data and analytics, integration, and AI, it empowers businesses to build smarter, more efficient, and more adaptable solutions.

Now, you can help shape what comes next.

Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now open, giving you as SAP customers and partners the opportunity to directly influence the future of SAP BTP. Collaborate with SAP product teams and developers, share feedback early, and help guide upcoming capabilities and improvements.

This isn’t just early insight - it’s your chance to impact the direction of enterprise technology.

Registration is open until March 13, 2026. 

Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 19th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP Developer Insights Survey

  • The 2026 SAP Developer Insights Survey is live now: https://sapinsights.eu.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_9prRAJk5ccYPMMe
  • 2025 Survey Report: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/the-2025-developer-insights-survey-the-report/ba-p/14323198

SAP RPT-1 Beta Available in the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

  • SAP RPT-1 (Beta): https://sap.github.io/ai-sdk/docs/js/rpt

SAP CodeJams in Australia and New Zealand

  • SAP CodeJam Group: https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam

SAP BTP, ABAP Environment Customer and Partner Roundtable

  • Event Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/live-demos-at-the-sap-btp-abap-environment-customer-amp-partner-roundtable/ba-p/14328427

Did You Know #4: Released Object in ABAP Development Tools

  • More Details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/7e27832a2d494e0b98359a7f1c6edefe.html?locale=en-US

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Developer Insights Survey

0:44 SAP RPT-1 Beta available in the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

1:17 SAP CodeJams in Australia and New Zealand

3:15 SAP BTP, ABAP Environment Customer and Partner Roundtable

4:07 DYK 4 Released Object in ADT

Transcription

00;00;00;00 - 00;00;05;06
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for February 19th, 2026.

00;00;05;09 - 00;00;09;22
Riley Rainey
Developers and SAP's annual Developer Insight survey has started live this

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;35;28
Riley Rainey
past Monday. You can get warmed up for it by reviewing Mamikee's blog posts in community. It's a complete report of the 2025 survey, contains all the data and also some key findings. There's tidbits on AI, popular frameworks help learning. It's all there. Have a look. This year we also have questions diving even deeper into those AI topics, we're keen to understand how you and your colleagues have been using AI for development in the past year.

00;00;36;01 - 00;00;44;26
Riley Rainey
We have links to the blog post and the survey in the section below. As always, your participation is key, so go check it all out. Thank you very much.

00;00;44;29 - 00;01;11;04
Kevin Riedelsheimer
The team behind the SAP cloud SDK for AI, released a new package to support the SAP Rapid-1 model. This package allows you to create a client to interface with the relational pre-trained transformer model. The package can simply be installed via npm, and is also currently only available for JavaScript. In case you haven't heard yet, but the Rapid-1 delivers accurate predictive insights from structured business data.

00;01;11;06 - 00;01;17;24
Kevin Riedelsheimer
This feature is currently in beta, but what are you waiting for? Try it out today.

00;01;17;27 - 00;01;19;02
Shilpa Shankar
We are live from

00;01;19;04 - 00;01;20;05
Rekha D R
Sydney.

00;01;20;07 - 00;01;21;04
Shilpa Shankar
Melbourne.

00;01;21;06 - 00;01;22;13
Rekha D R
Brisbane.

00;01;22;16 - 00;01;23;19
Shilpa Shankar
Auckland.

00;01;23;22 - 00;01;49;03
Shilpa Shankar
Hello SAP Community. We are in the second week of Australia New Zealand Roadshow and we just wrapped up ABAP Cloud CodeJam in this beautiful city of Auckland in New Zealand. We had lots of fun and lots of, technical discussions and ABAP Cloud topic because as many customers are moving to S/4 HANA and the feedback was tremendous.

00;01;49;05 - 00;01;57;07
Shilpa Shankar
Tomorrow we have another CodeJam related to Joule Studio in Auckland itself. Rekha can you highlight, what happened in Australia?

00;01;57;10 - 00;02;28;06
Rekha D R
Yeah, sure. So we started our week one in Australia, and we did, three CodeJams on Joule Studio topic. We started with Sydney and then we moved to Melbourne and then to Brisbane. There is a beautiful community, like all very technical people there. And we got to, try hands on with the customers and partners. And there is a very good response for Joule studio, product there.

00;02;28;09 - 00;02;54;28
Rekha D R
The community is very much excited for the, Joule and Joule Studio product and, yeah. And, in Brisbane we wrapped up, our, Australia trip with, SAP Stamtish (Meetup) with, wonderful SAP community, local community. So this way we had, lots of learning, lots of engagements with customers and partners. It was great, Shilpa.

00;02;55;01 - 00;03;14;09
Shilpa Shankar
Yeah, the local community meetup was good to meet, many local developers, in Brisbane. And, in case if you are interested to host, this CodeJam, you know what to do. Go to community.sap.com, go to SAP CodeJam group and then leave a discussion. We will get back to you. Thank you.

00;03;14;10 - 00;03;16;28
Rekha D R
Thank you. See Ya!

00;03;17;00 - 00;03;44;08
Mamikee Kanneh
Hi, everyone. Quick heads up. If you're working with SAP BTP, ABAP environment, the next Customer and partner roundtable is happening on March 3rd, 2026, from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. CET. This roundtable is a part of a monthly series that takes place on the first Tuesday of every month, and it's a great opportunity to stay up to date with the latest products, insights, updates, and live demos directly from the SAP team.

00;03;44;10 - 00;04;09;11
Mamikee Kanneh
They also publish everything centrally on SAP community, including the agendas, the slides, the recordings and updates from the past and current sessions. So it's a great resource even if you can't attend live now. If you want the full details and the link to join. Check out the blog post in the link below to learn more.

00;04;09;13 - 00;04;37;28
Shilpa Shankar
Did you know that released objects that are available for developer extensibility are displayed in the Project Explorer of ABAP Development Tools under Released Objects in ADT. Open the context menu of an ABAP project and choose new ABAP Repository Tree. This allows you to create a new tree and filter for released objects. You can expand the tree by various options such as application component, package, API state and so on.

00;04;38;00 - 00;04;44;21
Shilpa Shankar
All the released development objects will be grouped accordingly. Under the properties tab you can check the release contract.

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
When you're integrating launchpad content from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone and have an existing role model built on composite roles in your ABAP system, you've likely encountered the challenge of efficiently exposing these roles. The good news is there are two ways to expose composite roles from your ABAP backend to Work Zone, using the Common Data Model (CDM) content exposure. In this post we will break down what your options are, each with distinct technical implications.

Option 1: Direct Exposure of Composite Roles

With this approach, you select the composite role itself for exposure in the ABAP transaction /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP). The content of all underlying single roles is aggregated into the selected composite role, which is then exposed as single role to SAP BTP.

Key points

  • The consumer (Work Zone) sees the composite role as a single, unified role containing all the launchpad content of its component single roles.
  • The individual single roles inside the composite are not exposed separately.
  • Should you require separate access to the single roles beyond the unified composite role, you'll need to manually include each single role in the exposure selection.
  • Exposed roles are available in the BTP cockpit and can be assigned to users. However, IPS can optionally be used to automate role collection assignments and reduce administrative effort.
  • Direct exposure is available since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2023 or release 2022 with note 3325870

Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)

The second approach keeps your original role structure and the IPS ensures correct user to role assignment between the ABAP system and BTP.
You select only the single roles which contain launchpad content for exposure in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (not the composite role itself).
During content calculation, the system checks whether these single roles belong to any composite roles. If they do, it includes a reference to this relation in the exposed CDM content. The composite role itself is not exposed to Work Zone.

IPS handles the role assignment logic, ensuring users with composite roles get the correct roles assigned on BTP side.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about its composite role relationships, but those composite roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) ensures that when an user is assigned to a composite role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • If you expose only some single roles from a composite role, any other single roles within that composite are not automatically included. Users will only see apps from the exposed single roles in Work Zone not the full composite role content.
  • Indirect exposure is available since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022.

Next Steps

Both approaches are technically valid and work with CDM exposure version 1 and version 2.

If you're invested in IPS-driven provisioning the indirect approach gives you flexibility for Work Zone integration by keeping the existing role structure and automated user to role provisioning. Without IPS you have the option to directly expose composite roles and maintain user to role assignment on BTP side. 

In any case avoid creating dedicated roles solely for exposure purposes. Instead, use the same roles that are actively used by your local entry point (SAP Fiori Launchpad). This ensures consistency between your on-premise and cloud experiences, reduces administrative overhead, and maintains a single source of truth for your role-based content model.

entry.title: How SAP ESM + Document AI + S/4HANA Work Together to Automate Invoice Processing End‑to‑End
entry.content:
One of the most powerful capabilities in SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) is its ability to automate invoice processing the moment a customer or vendor sends an email. With SAP Document Information Extraction (Document AI) integrated into ESM, the journey from a raw invoice PDF to a validated posting in SAP S/4HANA Private or Public Edition becomes almost fully automated.
 

Step 1: The User Sends an Email with an Invoice

A customer or vendor sends an email to the SAP ESM dedicated email address configured for automatic case creation.

The invoice may be:

  • Attached as a PDF, JPEG, PNG, or
  • Embedded in the email body itself

SAP ESM picks up both.

Step 2: SAP ESM Automatically Creates a Case

Once the email reaches the ESM mailbox:

  • A new case is created automatically
  • The email body and all attachments are stored within the case
  • The attachments (PDFs, images) trigger SAP Document AI processing immediately

This eliminates manual case creation entirely.

Step 3: SAP Document AI Processes the Invoice

SAP Document AI retrieves the attachment from the case and begins extraction using OCR and machine learning.

It identifies:

  • Invoice number
  • Customer or vendor name
  • Address
  • Line items
  • Totals & taxes
  • Payment terms
  • Contract or PO references

Document AI also calculates a confidence score for each extracted field.
These scores help determine whether the data is reliable enough to auto‑post or requires user intervention.

Step 4: Validation Using Business Rules & Customer Framework

Once extraction is complete, SAP ESM applies:

  • Business rules
  • Customer validation frameworks
  • Mapping logic
  • Master data checks

These validations are essential before communicating with S/4HANA.

Examples:

  • Is the customer known in S/4?
  • Is the invoice number valid and not duplicated?
  • Does the PO exist?
  • Do line item totals match?

Only when the validations pass does ESM proceed to the next step.

Step 5: Invoice Number & Customer Auto‑Identification

If Document AI finds the invoice number, SAP ESM updates the line items and case fields:

  • invoiceNumber → updated
  • customerNumber → auto‑identified
  • isClearing → set according to logic (e.g., invoice found, ready to post)

This gives the ESM user immediate visibility:

  • Has the system recognized the invoice?
  • Has the customer been identified?
  • Is the invoice ready for posting?

Step 6: ESM Sends the Validated Invoice to S/4HANA via OData APIs

Once the case has all mandatory and validated data, the ESM user can trigger the next action:

“Post Invoice to S/4”

At this point:

  • ESM calls S/4HANA OData APIs
  • A draft or final FI invoice is created
  • Additional steps like clearing, matching, or workflow approvals occur inside S/4

This hands-off process ensures:

  • No manual entry in S/4
  • No data inconsistencies
  • Full auditability from source → document → posting

Step 7: ESM User Monitors and Completes the Process

Inside the ESM case, the user can now see:

  • Status of the posting
  • Document number returned from S/4
  • Any errors (e.g., master data inconsistencies)
  • New actions available for follow-up

They can then:

  • Update the customer
  • Add missing reference information
  • Re-trigger the posting
  • Close the case once processing is complete

Result: A Fully Streamlined, AI‑Driven Invoice Workflow

By integrating SAP ESM, Document AI, and S/4HANA, organizations benefit from:

✔ Zero manual invoice entry

✔ Auto-identification of customers and invoice data

✔ Early error detection using business rules

✔ Faster posting to S/4HANA

✔ End-to-end transparency in one central case

✔ Higher accuracy and reduced effort

This modernizes invoice handling from a traditionally manual process into an intelligent, automated workflow.

Learning Journey :  Business Documents in ESM

entry.title: When Business Asks for a Bicycle, Don't Build a Spaceship
entry.content:
When Business Asks for a Bicycle, Don't Build a Spaceship

I have developed a comprehensive playbook demonstrating how modern SAP tools and AI can deliver solutions in DAYS instead of MONTHS.

The shocking math:
- Traditional approach: 6 months, $650K in delayed value
- SAP tools + AI: 1 week, $25K in delayed value

This isn't just theory—it's a practical guide that covers:
- CDS Views for data modeling
- CAP for service generation
- Fiori Elements for instant UIs
- Integration Suite for connectivity
- AI to accelerate everything

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rajesh-putumbaka-456267101_sap-btp-ai-development-playbook-ugcPost-7431029390173696001-y7Yn?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABnYP8IBvlY4GDRmR8utVFLHXZ61gUvSa-0 
Download the full playbook (PDF attached) and share your thoughts: Have you encountered the "perfect is the enemy of good" trap in your SAP projects?
#SAP #DigitalTransformation #AI #SAPCommunity
